title: How Machiavelli's Florence bargained with Cesare Borgia for survival – Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer discusses Machiavelli as a misunderstood historical figure, focusing on his diplomatic career in Florence and how his experiences observing European rulers informed his political philosophy. She frames Machiavelli's decision to compile his insights into The Prince as a patriotic act comparable to Szilard's nuclear warning to FDR.
writeup: Historian Ada Palmer revisits Machiavelli as a misunderstood figure, centering his diplomatic work for Florence and his firsthand observations of European rulers, including the negotiations with Cesare Borgia that shaped his political thought. The conversation argues that those experiences fed directly into his political philosophy rather than the caricature attached to his name. The central claim: compiling his insights into The Prince was a patriotic act, framed as comparable to Szilard's nuclear warning to FDR.
